title: [Prochot Flow] Verify Prochot CBB Entry and Exit Flow
description:
(Sections with * are mandatory. You must fill them in, or say "Not Applicable" or "N/A" if you have seriously considered it and determined there's nothing to say. Remove these instructions and the explanatory text in each section as you fill things in.)

Flow *

use simics yaml and fuses in attachments to load simics, allows for freq scaling for the core

confirm core freq not Pm, rdmsr 0x198, bits 15:8 are the ratio

inject Prochot: oakstream.mb.mcp0.imh_die0.punit.punit[0]->prochot_trigger, prochot simics HAS PROCHOT modeling

rdmsr 0x198, confirm at Pm, 800-500Mhz

remove prochot 

rdmsr 0x198, confirm freq back to previous value,~4000Mhz


revised flow, 2025 WW46:

  • get pf curves for each gv domain
  • set each domain to highest ratio in the curve to ensure throttling at any power and cydyn point
  • get response power to determine the intended ceiling
  • confirm each domain is below that ceiling
  • release prochot and confirm freq above the prev ceiling 


CBB Entry Flow:

  • CBB asserts fast throttle to the Cores as soon as it sees the D2D Prochot wire asserted.
  • CBB Pcode reduces core frequencies as per PROCHOT_POWER_LIMITED_FREQ_LIMIT HPM message.
  • CBB Pcode de-asserts the fast throttle wire once the Core WP is applied.

Once the Prochot event passes.

CBB Exit Flow:

  • CBB Pcode removes any ceiling for the core frequencies as per PROCHOT_POWER_LIMITED_FREQ_LIMIT HPM message.


1. Read PROCHOT_RESPONSE_POWER 

2. Inject prochot using DFX or other script

3. Verify CBB core frequencies are reduced according to PROCHOT_RESPONSE_POWER

4. Release prochot signal

5. Verify CBB removes any core frequency ceiling


Configuration *

How does the platform need to be configured to obtain the desired coverage? For example: fuses, bios knobs, pre-test scripts, kernel/OS config. In the case of BIOS knobs, list all that impact this coverage, including both custom and default.
